skill.name: quality
skill.description: Validate Python code quality with formatting, linting, type checking, and pre-commit workflows.
skill.summary: Run consistent Python quality checks with Black, Ruff, BasedPyright, and pre-commit.
skill.author: ByronWilliamsCPA

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution, but the evidence is a bash code fence containing standard quality commands. The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive because that line is only descriptive metadata. No prompt injection, network access, secret access, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: External Quality Commands Require Local Execution
low_findings.description: The skill documents local commands for Black, Ruff, BasedPyright, and pre-commit. These are legitimate quality tools, but users should review repository configuration before running external commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command block is explicit and limited to standard development tooling. Risk is low because there is no dynamic input, network destination, or obfuscated command.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution points to Markdown fence delimiters, not executable Ruby code. No command substitution syntax is present at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both cited lines are Markdown code fence markers. The surrounding context confirms documentation, not executable source code.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm finding points to the skill description metadata. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption logic appears on that line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only descriptive text about code quality validation. There is no evidence of weak cryptography in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the assistant to use local quality tooling through shell commands. This is expected for the skill purpose, but it remains a publishable external command risk factor.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a clear bash command block with hardcoded quality commands. The commands are not malicious, but they can execute project-local tool configuration.

content.value_statement: Python projects often need repeatable formatting, linting, typing, and pre-commit checks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through standard quality workflows.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when formatting, linting, naming, type checking, or pre-commit workflows apply.
Provides Black and Ruff formatting commands for Python projects.
Provides Ruff lint commands, including an automatic fix option.
Provides BasedPyright type checking guidance for source directories.
Lists quality rule categories used by the project standards.
Explains that tests and scripts may use relaxed per-file rules.
limitations: Requires the target project to provide the referenced tools and configuration.
Does not define detailed rules for each linked workflow file.
Does not guarantee that automatic fixes preserve intended behavior.
Focuses on Python quality tooling, not general security review.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Python Pull Request
use_cases.description: Run formatting, linting, type checks, and pre-commit checks before requesting review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Repository Quality Checks
use_cases.description: Give contributors a consistent set of quality commands and rule categories.

use_cases.title: Triage CI Quality Failures
use_cases.description: Map failing lint, formatting, typing, or hook output to the expected local workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Quality Checks
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for checking a Python change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the quality skill to format and lint this Python project. Show me the commands you plan to run first.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Formatting and Lint Issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for applying safe automatic fixes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the quality skill to run the formatter and Ruff fix workflow. Summarize any files that changed and any issues left unresolved.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Types and Hooks
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow before merging a branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the quality skill to run type checking and all pre-commit hooks. Explain each failure and the smallest safe fix.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Quality Standards
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for maintainers reviewing project rules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the quality skill to compare this repository against its stated Python quality standards. Identify gaps in formatting, linting, typing, and per-file ignores.

output_examples.input: Check whether my Python branch is ready for review.
output: Runs formatting and lint checks first.
Runs type checking on the source directory.
Reports changed files and remaining failures in plain language.

output_examples.input: Ruff found several naming and logging problems.
output: Explains which findings can be fixed automatically.
Lists manual follow-up items for private member access and logging format issues.
Recommends reproducing the all-files hook run locally.
Separates formatting failures from type checking failures.
Suggests a minimal order for resolving failures.

best_practices: Review project tool configuration before running automatic fixes.
Run formatting before linting so later checks use stable file content.
Commit formatting-only changes separately when they affect many files.
anti_patterns: Do not run automatic fixes without checking the resulting diff.
Do not ignore type checker failures without documenting the reason.
Do not relax per-file rules broadly when a targeted ignore is enough.

faq.question: What languages does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Python projects that use Black, Ruff, BasedPyright, and pre-commit.

faq.question: Can it fix code automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide formatter and Ruff fix commands, but users should review all changed files.

faq.question: Does it install missing tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill assumes the project already provides the required tools through its environment.

faq.question: Does it replace CI checks?
faq.answer: No. It helps run local checks that should match CI when configuration is consistent.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low external command risk from documented local quality commands.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
